甲.wiki_summary: Pictogram of the protective hard shell, carapace, or scales of an animal, or a suit of armor made of connected plates.
甲.note: Wiktionary reads 甲 as a carapace or suit of armor (Proto-Sino-Tibetan *krap "shell/shield"), not a sprouting seed shell as stated in our data.

虫.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound (triplication of 虫 'snake; insect') — three 虫 stacked to form 蟲.
虫.note: The simplified 虫 is itself a pictograph (snake), but its traditional form 蟲 is an ideogrammic compound (3×虫). Our formation describes the component 虫, not the traditional character 蟲.

言.wiki_summary: Ideogram (指事) from 口 plus ▽ (possibly original form of 弋, marking location); later mixed with 舌/辛/䇂
言.note: Both classify as simple indicative. Wiktionary describes the marker as a tongue-position indicator above 口 (mouth), slightly different from our "tongue speaking" reading.

广.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 广 + phonetic 黃 (OC *ɡʷaːŋ); 廣 reconstructed as OC *kʷaːŋʔ
广.note: Wiktionary classifies 廣 as a phono-semantic compound: 广 (cliff/shelter, semantic) + 黃 (phonetic). Our data treats it as a simple pictograph of a mountain-side shelter.

食.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 亼 (mouth) + 皀 (bowl of rice on a stand)
食.note: Wiktionary classifies 食 as an ideogrammic compound: 亼 (mouth) + 皀 (food in a vessel). Our data incorrectly assigns 皂 as a phonetic component, making it phono-semantic.

又.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a right hand; original form of 右
又.note: Both classify as pictograph. Wiktionary clarifies the depicted object is a right hand (original form of 右), not "repetition" as our note implies.

足.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a leg, similar to 疋.
足.note: Wiktionary reads 足 as a unified pictograph of a leg and foot, not a compound of separate semantic components (止 + knee).

干.wiki_summary: Originally 榦, phono-semantic compound: phonetic 倝 + semantic 木 (tree); later 木 replaced by phonetic 干.
干.note: Simplified 干 merges three traditional chars: 幹 (trunk/manage, phono-semantic: 倝+木), 乾 (dry, phono-semantic), and 干 (shield/offend, pictograph). Our formation describes the 干 (shield) sense only.

甘.wiki_summary: Indicative (指事): depicts something sweet inside an open mouth.
甘.note: Wiktionary classifies 甘 as a simple indicative (指事): the stroke inside 口 is an abstract position marker for "something sweet in the mouth," not a second semantic component making it a compound ideograph.

十.wiki_summary: No explicit formation type given; glyph origin describes a single vertical stroke in oracle bone script, with theories of pictogram (needle borrowed for numeral) or knotted-cord form — 指事 not mentioned
十.note: Formation origin uncertain. Wiktionary does not confirm the indicative reading; theories include a borrowed pictogram of a needle or a knotted cord used for counting.

皮.wiki_summary: Pictogram: depicts a hand (又) stripping fur from an animal pelt; the pelt moved to top-left and became abstracted
皮.note: Wiktionary classifies 皮 as a pictograph: a unified depiction of a hand stripping an animal pelt, not an ideogrammic compound of independent semantic parts.

亡.wiki_summary: Ideogram (指事): a line on a knife depicting its cutting edge; original form of 芒.
亡.note: Wiktionary classifies 亡 as a simple indicative: a mark on a knife blade indicating the hidden/absent edge — original form of 芒. Our data's compound ideograph analysis (入+乚 = person hiding) is a later folk reading.

旦.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound (形聲): semantic 日 (sun/day) + phonetic 丁; Shuowen's sun-over-horizon reading is considered folk etymology.
旦.note: Type mismatch: we classify as simple_indicative based on the traditional sun-over-horizon reading; Wiktionary classifies as phono_semantic_compound with 丁 as phonetic component, treating the indicative reading as folk etymology.

示.note: Wiktionary classifies 示 as a pictograph: a direct depiction of a ritual altar or spirit tablet, not an ideogrammic compound of separate semantic parts.

古.wiki_summary: Indicative (指事): shield + 口 (mouth) indicating 'strong/firm'; original form of 固
古.note: Wiktionary classifies 古 as a simple indicative, with the top element reading as a contracted shield (not 十), meaning "firm/strong" — original form of 固. The "ten mouths = ancient" folk reading in our data is not etymologically supported.

申.wiki_summary: Pictogram (象形): depicts a bolt of lightning; original form of 電 (lightning) and 神 (deity)
申.note: Wiktionary classifies 申 as a pictograph of a lightning bolt, not an indicative of an upright bound body. The lightning interpretation aligns with its original meaning "9th earthly branch" and related character 電.

舌.wiki_summary: Pictogram (象形): a snake's tongue emerging upward from a mouth (口)
舌.note: Wiktionary reads 舌 as a pictograph of a snake's forked tongue above a mouth — a unified image, not an ideogrammic compound of 口 + 干.

之.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 止 (foot) + 一 (starting place) — originally meaning 'to go'
之.note: Wiktionary identifies the components as 止 (foot) + 一 (ground), meaning "to go/proceed." Our data's sprouting-plant description misidentifies the character; that imagery belongs to 生 or 屮.

黑.wiki_summary: Pictogram (象形): depicts a covered head or face in oracle bone script; dots added in bronze inscriptions
黑.note: Wiktionary classifies 黑 as a pictograph of a face or head covered in soot/markings. Our chimney+fire compound reading is not supported by glyph analysis.

夕.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a moon crescent; compare ancient variant 月 (full moon).
夕.note: Wiktionary classifies 夕 as a pictograph of a crescent moon — same imagery as our note, but formation type is pictograph not simple indicative.

韦.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 舛 + 囗, depicting footsteps around an enclosure; possibly original form of 圍/衛, borrowed for 'tanned leather'.
韦.note: Wiktionary classifies 韋 as an ideogrammic compound: 舛 (contrary footsteps) + 囗 (enclosure) = feet going opposite ways around a wall. Our data treats 囗 as a phonetic, making it phono-semantic — incorrect.

谷.wiki_summary: Wiktionary page for traditional form 穀 (grain): phono-semantic compound, semantic 禾 (grain) + phonetic 𣪊.
谷.note: Simplified 谷 covers two unrelated traditional characters: 谷 (valley, ideogrammic compound) and 穀 (grain, phono-semantic: 禾+𣪊). These were merged in simplification; our formation addresses only the valley sense.

向.wiki_summary: Wiktionary page for 向: ideogrammic compound 宀 (house) + 口 (mouth/opening), representing echoes in a place; original form of 響, later borrowed for 嚮 (toward). Page for 嚮 shows it is ⿱鄉向, a further derived form.
向.note: Wiktionary classifies 向 as an ideogrammic compound: 宀 (dwelling) + 口 (opening/window) = a north-facing window. Our data calls it a pictograph.

垂.wiki_summary: Pictogram: 𠂹 (flowers hanging from a plant) + 土 (earth), depicting plants drooping downward.
垂.note: Wiktionary classifies 垂 as a pictograph of hanging flowers drooping toward the ground — a unified image. Our data incorrectly assigns a phono-semantic structure (土 semantic + 垂 phonetic, which is circular).

青.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 生 (growth of plants), phonetic 井; Shuowen's ideogrammic reading (生+丹) noted but not adopted.
青.note: Wiktionary (citing Zhang 2022) classifies 青 as a phono-semantic compound: 生 (semantic, growth/plant) + 井 (phonetic). The Shuowen compound ideograph reading (生+丹) is an older analysis.

走.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a running man; 止 (foot) added later; top component is 夭.
走.note: Wiktionary classifies 走 as a pictograph of a running person — a unified pictorial image, not an ideogrammic compound of person + foot parts.

士.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a war axe; later took meanings of soldier and intellectual; related to 王 and 圭.
士.note: Wiktionary classifies 士 as a pictograph of a war axe. The "一+十 = a man of virtue" compound reading in our data is a folk etymology not supported by glyph analysis.

旨.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound (primary): 匕 (spoon) used to eat or drink something delicious. Shuowen gives secondary phono-semantic reading: phonetic 匕 + semantic 甘 ('delicious').
旨.note: Wiktionary's primary classification is ideogrammic compound: 匕 (spoon) + 甘 (sweet) = something delicious eaten with a spoon. The phono-semantic reading (匕 phonetic) is Shuowen's secondary account.

合.wiki_summary: Wiktionary page for traditional form 閤: phono-semantic compound (OC *kuːb): semantic 門 ('door') + phonetic 合. This character means 'side door/small room', not the simplex 合.
合.note: 閤 is a derived character (門+合, phono-semantic) distinct from 合 itself. 合 (ideogrammic compound: 亼+口 = lid closing over mouth) is correct; but the traditional form listed in our data (閤) is a separate word meaning "side door."

束.wiki_summary: Ideogram (指事) – a bag tied at both ends (earlier forms often vertically symmetric); related to 東 and 柬.
束.note: Wiktionary classifies 束 as a simple indicative: a depiction of a bag tied at both ends, with the binding marks as position indicators — not an ideogrammic compound of 囗 (rope) + 木 (wood).

五.wiki_summary: Originally written 𠄡 (㐅 with bars top and bottom); original meaning unclear. Wiktionary's leading theory cites it as a pictogram (palm lines from extending fingers), with no consensus on indicative classification.
五.note: Wiktionary's leading theory classifies 五 as a pictograph (palm lines or a knotted cord representing the number 5), not a simple indicative. Origin uncertain.

莫.wiki_summary: Pictogram of the sun (日) sinking into the bushes (屮), representing dusk; the bottom was later distorted into 大.
莫.note: Wiktionary classifies 莫 as a pictograph: sun sinking into vegetation = dusk, a unified visual image. Our data classifies it as an ideogrammic compound of 日 + 茻 — same imagery but different classification.

千.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound (OC *sn̥ʰiːn): semantic 一 ('one, signifying a number') + phonetic 人. Note: Wiktionary page for trad form 韆 (swing/千秋) is a different character.
千.note: Wiktionary classifies 千 as a phono-semantic compound: 一 (semantic) + 人 (phonetic). Our simple indicative reading is not supported. Note: the traditional form 韆 listed in our data means "swing" (千秋), not the numeral — an incorrect trad mapping.

斗.wiki_summary: Wiktionary page for traditional form 鬥: pictogram of two figures face-to-face fighting with bare hands, hair bristled. This is a completely different character from 斗 (ladle/measure).
斗.note: Simplified 斗 merges two unrelated traditional characters: 斗 (ladle/measure, pictograph of a handled vessel) and 鬥 (fight, pictograph of two people wrestling). These are completely different characters with different etymologies.

的.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound with semantic component 白 ('white') and phonetic component 勺; the original form was 旳 meaning 'bright'.
的.note: Phono-semantic compound with semantic component 白 ('white') and phonetic component 勺; the original form was 旳 meaning 'bright'.

了.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound (形聲): semantic 目 ('eye') + phonetic 尞, referring to the character 瞭 meaning 'to see clearly'.
了.note: Phono-semantic compound (形聲): semantic 目 ('eye') + phonetic 尞, referring to the character 瞭 meaning 'to see clearly'.

有.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound with phonetic 又 and semantic 肉 ('meat'), not a compound ideograph of a hand holding meat as folk etymology claims.
有.note: Phono-semantic compound with phonetic 又 and semantic 肉 ('meat'), not a compound ideograph of a hand holding meat as folk etymology claims.

我.wiki_summary: Pictogram (象形) depicting a trident-like weapon or three-bladed polearm, later borrowed phonetically (假借) for the first-person pronoun.
我.note: Pictogram (象形) depicting a trident-like weapon or three-bladed polearm, later borrowed phonetically (假借) for the first-person pronoun.

中.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a flagpole (interpreted as a drum-bearing pole placed in the center of a field); Shuowen alternatively describes it as a vertical stroke through 口 indicating center.
中.note: Pictogram of a flagpole (interpreted as a drum-bearing pole placed in the center of a field); Shuowen alternatively describes it as a vertical stroke through 口 indicating center.

为.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 又 ('hand') + 象 ('elephant') — a hand holding/leading an elephant; 又 later became 爪.
为.note: Ideogrammic compound: 又 ('hand') + 象 ('elephant') — a hand holding/leading an elephant; 又 later became 爪.

国.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound; the k-prefix derivation of 域 ('boundary; territory'), cognate with 閾 and 囿.
国.note: Phono-semantic compound; the k-prefix derivation of 域 ('boundary; territory'), cognate with 閾 and 囿.

出.wiki_summary: Wiktionary's 齣 entry is a theatrical classifier character (phono-semantic: 齒 + 句), unrelated to the pictographic 出 ('to exit'); the trad field 齣 does not correspond to the formation described.
出.note: Wiktionary's 齣 entry is a theatrical classifier character (phono-semantic: 齒 + 句), unrelated to the pictographic 出 ('to exit'); the trad field 齣 does not correspond to the formation described.

会.wiki_summary: Pictogram: two open mouths with a food container in the middle, representing gathering/closing.
会.note: Pictogram: two open mouths with a food container in the middle, representing gathering/closing.

可.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 口 + phonetic 丂, with reference to exhaled air; original meaning possibly 'to sing'.
可.note: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 口 + phonetic 丂, with reference to exhaled air; original meaning possibly 'to sing'.

也.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a child (子) with mouth (口) open crying; arms were removed in bronze inscriptions and components fused in the modern form.
也.note: Pictogram of a child (子) with mouth (口) open crying; arms were removed in bronze inscriptions and components fused in the modern form.

着.wiki_summary: Wiktionary classifies 著 as a phono-semantic compound (形聲): semantic 艸 (grass/plant) + phonetic 者; 着 is a simplified/vulgar form of 著.
着.note: Wiktionary classifies 著 as a phono-semantic compound (形聲): semantic 艸 (grass/plant) + phonetic 者; 着 is a simplified/vulgar form of 著.

发.wiki_summary: Originally a pictogram of a bow firing an arrow; the phonetic component is 癹 (not 癶 as our note states), and the form is not from 殳.
发.note: Originally a pictogram of a bow firing an arrow; the phonetic component is 癹 (not 癶 as our note states), and the form is not from 殳.

后.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound depicting a bound/lagging foot (幺 + 夊) with walking radical 彳, meaning 'behind/after'; our note's component analysis (人 + 口) is incorrect.
后.note: Ideogrammic compound depicting a bound/lagging foot (幺 + 夊) with walking radical 彳, meaning 'behind/after'; our note's component analysis (人 + 口) is incorrect.

作.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: 亻 (person) as semantic, 乍 as phonetic component meaning 'to do/make'.
作.note: Phono-semantic compound: 亻 (person) as semantic, 乍 as phonetic component meaning 'to do/make'.

用.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound formed by adding a handle to 同, which also serves as the phonetic component.
用.note: Phono-semantic compound formed by adding a handle to 同, which also serves as the phonetic component.

事.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 又 ('hand') + 中 ('flagpole with a drum'), meaning 'flag in hand > to perform one's job'.
事.note: Ideogrammic compound: 又 ('hand') + 中 ('flagpole with a drum'), meaning 'flag in hand > to perform one's job'.

成.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 戊 + phonetic 丁, with original meaning relating to 'city walls'.
成.note: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 戊 + phonetic 丁, with original meaning relating to 'city walls'.

去.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 大 (man) + 口 (mouth/object); top simplified to 土, bottom to 厶.
去.note: Ideogrammic compound: 大 (man) + 口 (mouth/object); top simplified to 土, bottom to 厶.

法.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 氵 (water) + semantic 廌 (antelope) + abbreviated phonetic 盍; 廌 was later omitted.
法.note: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 氵 (water) + semantic 廌 (antelope) + abbreviated phonetic 盍; 廌 was later omitted.

如.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: phonetic 女 + semantic 口 (mouth); phonetic component also carries ideographic suggestion of a woman obeying an order.
如.note: Phono-semantic compound: phonetic 女 + semantic 口 (mouth); phonetic component also carries ideographic suggestion of a woman obeying an order.

同.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a pipe, original form of 筒; 口 was added later to mark the opening.
同.note: Pictogram of a pipe, original form of 筒; 口 was added later to mark the opening.

没.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 水 (water) + phonetic 𠬛.
没.note: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 水 (water) + phonetic 𠬛.

面.wiki_summary: Wiktionary's page for trad 麵 (flour/noodles) classifies it as a phono-semantic compound: semantic 麥 (wheat) + phonetic 面; note that 面 (face) and 麵 (noodles) are distinct characters and the trad field appears to be incorrect.
面.note: Wiktionary's page for trad 麵 (flour/noodles) classifies it as a phono-semantic compound: semantic 麥 (wheat) + phonetic 面; note that 面 (face) and 麵 (noodles) are distinct characters and the trad field appears to be incorrect.

起.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound with semantic 走 (walk/move) and phonetic 巳.
起.note: Phono-semantic compound with semantic 走 (walk/move) and phonetic 巳.

部.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: phonetic 咅 (OC *pʰɯʔ) + semantic 邑; originally referring to a place in today's Gansu.
部.note: Phono-semantic compound: phonetic 咅 (OC *pʰɯʔ) + semantic 邑; originally referring to a place in today's Gansu.

其.wiki_summary: Pictogram originally depicting a basket; 丌 (stand) was later added beneath, then the character was phonetically borrowed as a pronoun, with derivative 箕 retaining the original meaning.
其.note: Pictogram originally depicting a basket; 丌 (stand) was later added beneath, then the character was phonetically borrowed as a pronoun, with derivative 箕 retaining the original meaning.

前.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: originally 歬, combining 止 (foot) and 舟 (boat) to represent a foot on a boat moving forward; 刀 was later added, making this the original form of 剪.
前.note: Ideogrammic compound: originally 歬, combining 止 (foot) and 舟 (boat) to represent a foot on a boat moving forward; 刀 was later added, making this the original form of 剪.

只.wiki_summary: Wiktionary entry for traditional 隻: ideogrammic compound of 隹 (short-tailed bird) + 又 (hand), depicting one bird in hand, contrasting with 雙 (two birds).
只.note: Wiktionary entry for traditional 隻: ideogrammic compound of 隹 (short-tailed bird) + 又 (hand), depicting one bird in hand, contrasting with 雙 (two birds).

从.wiki_summary: Wiktionary classifies traditional 從 as a phono-semantic compound: semantic 辵 (walk/follow) + phonetic 从.
从.note: Wiktionary classifies traditional 從 as a phono-semantic compound: semantic 辵 (walk/follow) + phonetic 从.

军.wiki_summary: Wiktionary's primary classification of 軍 is phono-semantic compound (semantic 車 chariot + phonetic element); the Shuowen Jiezi offers an alternative ideogrammic reading (cart + surround).
军.note: Wiktionary's primary classification of 軍 is phono-semantic compound (semantic 車 chariot + phonetic element); the Shuowen Jiezi offers an alternative ideogrammic reading (cart + surround).

者.wiki_summary: Pictogram originally depicting a sugarcane plant with stems and leaves, later phonetically borrowed for the nominalizing suffix meaning 'one who'.
者.note: Pictogram originally depicting a sugarcane plant with stems and leaves, later phonetically borrowed for the nominalizing suffix meaning 'one who'.

无.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a person dancing with ox tails in both hands to pray for rain; later borrowed for the meaning 'have no', with 舞 adopted for the original sense.
无.note: Pictogram of a person dancing with ox tails in both hands to pray for rain; later borrowed for the meaning 'have no', with 舞 adopted for the original sense.

它.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a snake (now written as 蛇); later borrowed via jiajie for the third-person pronoun.
它.note: Pictogram of a snake (now written as 蛇); later borrowed via jiajie for the third-person pronoun.

与.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound with two phonetic components: 舁 (OC *la) and 牙 (OC *ŋraː), both serving as phonetics.
与.note: Phono-semantic compound with two phonetic components: 舁 (OC *la) and 牙 (OC *ŋraː), both serving as phonetics.

民.wiki_summary: Pictogram: oracle bone script forms depict an eye pierced by a cross-shaped sharp implement.
民.note: Pictogram: oracle bone script forms depict an eye pierced by a cross-shaped sharp implement.

知.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 大 (adult) + 口 (mouth) + 子 (child) – an adult transmitting knowledge to a child; originally the same character as 智.
知.note: Ideogrammic compound: 大 (adult) + 口 (mouth) + 子 (child) – an adult transmitting knowledge to a child; originally the same character as 智.

全.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 入 + 玉 (jade) – a whole, unblemished piece of jade.
全.note: Ideogrammic compound: 入 + 玉 (jade) – a whole, unblemished piece of jade.

关.wiki_summary: Wiktionary labels 關 as a phono-semantic compound (形聲, OC *kroːn): semantic 門 ('door') + phonetic 𢇅 (two wooden sticks forming a latch), noting the bronze form may already carry a phonetic element.
关.note: Wiktionary labels 關 as a phono-semantic compound (形聲, OC *kroːn): semantic 門 ('door') + phonetic 𢇅 (two wooden sticks forming a latch), noting the bronze form may already carry a phonetic element.

正.wiki_summary: Wiktionary labels 正 as a phono-semantic compound (形聲, OC *tjeŋ): phonetic 丁 + semantic 止 ('foot').
正.note: Wiktionary labels 正 as a phono-semantic compound (形聲, OC *tjeŋ): phonetic 丁 + semantic 止 ('foot').

外.wiki_summary: Wiktionary labels 外 as a phono-semantic compound (形聲, OC *ŋʷaːds): semantic 卜 + phonetic 月.
外.note: Wiktionary labels 外 as a phono-semantic compound (形聲, OC *ŋʷaːds): semantic 卜 + phonetic 月.

间.wiki_summary: Wiktionary describes 間 as a variant of 閒, which is an ideogrammic compound (會意): 門 ('door') + 月/日 ('moon'/'sun') – moonlight or daylight peeking through a door.
间.note: Wiktionary describes 間 as a variant of 閒, which is an ideogrammic compound (會意): 門 ('door') + 月/日 ('moon'/'sun') – moonlight or daylight peeking through a door.

最.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: originally 㝡, semantic 冃 (roof/cap) + phonetic 取; later modified with distinguishing marks to mean 'the most'.
最.note: Phono-semantic compound: originally 㝡, semantic 冃 (roof/cap) + phonetic 取; later modified with distinguishing marks to mean 'the most'.

重.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound and ideogrammic compound: semantic 人 (man) + phonetic 東 (bag) + 土 (earth), depicting a man carrying a heavy bag standing on ground.
重.note: Phono-semantic compound and ideogrammic compound: semantic 人 (man) + phonetic 東 (bag) + 土 (earth), depicting a man carrying a heavy bag standing on ground.

文.wiki_summary: Ideogram (指事): depicts a man with a painted or tattooed chest; original meaning 'tattoo/pattern' evolved to 'written character', 'literature', and 'refined/cultured'.
文.note: Ideogram (指事): depicts a man with a painted or tattooed chest; original meaning 'tattoo/pattern' evolved to 'written character', 'literature', and 'refined/cultured'.

美.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a person (大) wearing a feathered or ram's-horn headdress (written 羊); the ideogrammic compound reading is secondary.
美.note: Pictogram of a person (大) wearing a feathered or ram's-horn headdress (written 羊); the ideogrammic compound reading is secondary.

见.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 目 ('eye') + 卩 ('kneeling person').
见.note: Ideogrammic compound: 目 ('eye') + 卩 ('kneeling person').

或.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound (形聲): semantic 戈 + phonetic 員 (*wən, 'circle').
或.note: Phono-semantic compound (形聲): semantic 戈 + phonetic 員 (*wən, 'circle').

己.wiki_summary: Pictogram (象形): depicts a silk rope for binding objects; original form of 紀.
己.note: Pictogram (象形): depicts a silk rope for binding objects; original form of 紀.

斯.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound (會意字): 其 (sieve) + 斤 (axe); original meaning 'to divide/break with an axe'.
斯.note: Ideogrammic compound (會意字): 其 (sieve) + 斤 (axe); original meaning 'to divide/break with an axe'.

信.wiki_summary: Wiktionary classifies 信 as phono-semantic: phonetic 人 + semantic 言 ('words'); the Shuowen's ideogrammic reading is noted as an alternative interpretation.
信.note: Wiktionary classifies 信 as phono-semantic: phonetic 人 + semantic 言 ('words'); the Shuowen's ideogrammic reading is noted as an alternative interpretation.

表.wiki_summary: Wiktionary's page for 錶 (the trad used) describes it as a differentiated character coined c. 1900 from 表 by adding the metal radical 釒 to specify 'watch/timepiece'; the compound_ideograph analysis (衣+毛) belongs to 表, not 錶.
表.note: Wiktionary's page for 錶 (the trad used) describes it as a differentiated character coined c. 1900 from 表 by adding the metal radical 釒 to specify 'watch/timepiece'; the compound_ideograph analysis (衣+毛) belongs to 表, not 錶.

老.wiki_summary: Wiktionary classifies 老 as an ideogrammic compound: 人 ('man') + 毛 ('hair') + 匕 ('cane'), depicting an old man with long hair leaning on a cane.
老.note: Wiktionary classifies 老 as an ideogrammic compound: 人 ('man') + 毛 ('hair') + 匕 ('cane'), depicting an old man with long hair leaning on a cane.

世.wiki_summary: Wiktionary describes 世 as a phonetic borrowing (假借) derived from 葉 (leaf) via iterative devoicing, representing successive foliages extended to mean 'generations' — not a simple indicative character.
世.note: Wiktionary describes 世 as a phonetic borrowing (假借) derived from 葉 (leaf) via iterative devoicing, representing successive foliages extended to mean 'generations' — not a simple indicative character.

次.wiki_summary: Wiktionary classifies 次 as a pictogram depicting a person exhaling, noting the Shuowen's phono-semantic analysis does not accord with the ancient forms.
次.note: Wiktionary classifies 次 as a pictogram depicting a person exhaling, noting the Shuowen's phono-semantic analysis does not accord with the ancient forms.

度.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 又 (hand) + phonetic 石 (later corrupted to 𲟁), relating to hand-span measurement; Shuowen's 庶-as-phonetic analysis is noted but not the primary reconstruction.
度.note: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 又 (hand) + phonetic 石 (later corrupted to 𲟁), relating to hand-span measurement; Shuowen's 庶-as-phonetic analysis is noted but not the primary reconstruction.

东.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a full container tied at both ends; the sun-in-tree (日+木) reading is described as folk etymology.
东.note: Pictogram of a full container tied at both ends; the sun-in-tree (日+木) reading is described as folk etymology.

比.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 人 (man) + 人 – two people juxtaposed.
比.note: Ideogrammic compound: 人 (man) + 人 – two people juxtaposed.

员.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 〇 indicating the round top edge of a 鼎 (ritual vessel) – originally meaning round/circular.
员.note: Ideogrammic compound: 〇 indicating the round top edge of a 鼎 (ritual vessel) – originally meaning round/circular.

真.wiki_summary: Wiktionary gives no explicit formation-type label but treats 真 as a phono-semantic compound, possibly connected with 貞 (OC *teŋ); no ideogrammic-compound analysis is given.
真.note: Wiktionary gives no explicit formation-type label but treats 真 as a phono-semantic compound, possibly connected with 貞 (OC *teŋ); no ideogrammic-compound analysis is given.

处.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: phonetic 虍 + semantic 夂 + semantic 几 (table).
处.note: Phono-semantic compound: phonetic 虍 + semantic 夂 + semantic 几 (table).

各.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: 夂 (sole of foot) + 口 — to come, to enter; original form of 格.
各.note: Ideogrammic compound: 夂 (sole of foot) + 口 — to come, to enter; original form of 格.

入.wiki_summary: Ideogram (指事): an arrowhead shape indicating 'to enter'.
入.note: Ideogram (指事): an arrowhead shape indicating 'to enter'.

几.wiki_summary: Wiktionary page is for trad 幾: Ideogrammic compound: 𢆶 (silk threads; little things) + 戍 (to guard against) — watching over the fine details.
几.note: Wiktionary page is for trad 幾: Ideogrammic compound: 𢆶 (silk threads; little things) + 戍 (to guard against) — watching over the fine details.

平.wiki_summary: Wiktionary lists 平 as a Pictogram (象形), possibly depicting waterweed on water or a balance scale; no simple_indicative classification is given.
平.note: Wiktionary lists 平 as a Pictogram (象形), possibly depicting waterweed on water or a balance scale; no simple_indicative classification is given.

系.wiki_summary: Wiktionary classifies 繫 as a Phono-semantic compound with 糸 (thread) as semantic and 毄 as phonetic component.
系.note: Wiktionary classifies 繫 as a Phono-semantic compound with 糸 (thread) as semantic and 毄 as phonetic component.

气.wiki_summary: Wiktionary classifies 氣 as a Phono-semantic compound with 米 (rice) as semantic and 气 as phonetic, originally denoting stored grain.
气.note: Wiktionary classifies 氣 as a Phono-semantic compound with 米 (rice) as semantic and 气 as phonetic, originally denoting stored grain.

活.wiki_summary: Wiktionary confirms 活 as a Phono-semantic compound with 氵 as semantic and 舌 (evolved from 𠯑) as phonetic; our note incorrectly names 昏 as the phonetic.
活.note: Wiktionary confirms 活 as a Phono-semantic compound with 氵 as semantic and 舌 (evolved from 𠯑) as phonetic; our note incorrectly names 昏 as the phonetic.

尔.wiki_summary: Wiktionary classifies 爾 as a Pictogram (象形) depicting cloth on a loom with crossing threads, later phonetically borrowed to mean 'you'.
尔.note: Wiktionary classifies 爾 as a Pictogram (象形) depicting cloth on a loom with crossing threads, later phonetically borrowed to mean 'you'.
